english: ra-lá-rnams (ra la rnams) 。púl-tsul ('phul tshul) ni the manner in which prefixes are joined with words beginning with s, r or l; rkyań-。púl (rkyang 'phul) words beginning with a simple consonant (to which also ya- (ya), ra- (ra), and la-tags (la btags) are reckoned), preceded by a prefix; brtsegs-。púl (brtsegs 'phul), words beginning with two consonants and a prefix e.g. bsku (bsku) Gram.

I. v. the preceding article. — II. vb. 1. = 。búl-ba ('bul ba), to give. — 2. to push, to jostle; *púl-túg gyáb-čè* (phul thug rgyab ce), to push with the fist, with the trunk, (of elephants) etc., W.; grú-mor (gru mor) 。púl-rdeg čig byéd-pa ('phul rdeg cig byed pa), to jostle with the elbow Mil.; vulgo *púl-dag (phul dag) or tag* (thag) W., *。púl-tsúg* ('phul tshug) C.

pf. 。páńs ('phangs), fut. (and frq. for the pres.) 。páń ('phang), imp. póń (phong), páńs (phangs), 1. to throw, to cast, to fling; nám-mka-la (nam mkha' la) into the air Dzl.; kór-bar ('khor bar), to throw into the orb of transmigration Mil.; dmyál-bar (dmyal bar), to cast into hell Thgy.; *k̃a páń - čè* (kha 'phang ce), to shovel snow (out of the road, from the roof); *páń-te bór-ra tsíg-te bor* ('phang te bor ra tshig te bor) am I to throw down the wood, or pile it up? W.; *pu páń-čé* (pu 'phang ce) to cast the hair W.; hence 。páń ('phang), spindle, and 。pén-siń ('phen shing), acc. to Sch., a weaver's shuttle (it being flung). — 2. to fire off, to discharge, to let fly, mda (mda'), an arrow, gźan-la (gzhan la), at another Dzl.; 。pén-duń ('phen mdung) dart, javelin Stg.; to shoot, 。pén-mi śés-pa ('phen mi shes pa), W. *páń-mi-śes-k̃an* ('phang mi shes mkhan), one that does not know how to shoot. — 3. Sch. 。pén-pa btań-ba ('phen pa btang ba) 'to intend, to have a mind, to think upon, to consider', (yet in the only passage, in which I met with the word, in Thgr., the above meaning does not seem applicable).

I. vb. pf. pél (phel) (ཕེལ་) vb. n. to spél-ba (spel ba), opp. to 。grib-pa ('grib pa), 1. to increase, augment, multiply, enlarge, frq.; *sum lan nyi-la tsam pél* (sum lan nyi la tsam phel) how many are two times three? W.; 。pél-。grib-kyi dbáń-gis ('phel 'grib kyi dbang gis) in consequence of the increase and decrease Gram.; 。pél-。grib-nád ('phel 'grib nad), prob. diseases arising from an excess or deficiency of humours Wdn. — 2. to improve, to grow better, bsam-gtán (bsam gtan) or tugs-dám (thugs dam) 。pél-ba yin ('phel ba yin) meditation has improved, has proceeded better Mil. — II. sbst., Sch. also 。pél-k̃a ('phel kha), 1. increase. 2. development S.g.

pf. and imp. 。pos ('phos), prop. intrans. to spó-ba (spo ba), = gnas-spó-ba (gnas spo ba); 1. to change place, shift, migrate frq.; myur-du 。pos-śig (myur du 'phos shig), go speedily elsewhere! Dzl.; in a more general sense to change, 。pó-méd bdé-ba ('pho med bde ba) changeless happiness; in a similar sense 。pó-。gyur-méd-pai rnal-byór ('pho 'gyur med pa'i rnal 'byor) Mil.; yet frq. also vb. a.: k̃ú-ba yań 。pó yań 。pó byás śiń (khu ba yang 'pho yang 'pho byas shing) pouring off the gravy again and again Pth.; very frq. tse 。pó-ba (tshe 'pho ba), či-。pó-ba ('chi 'pho ba), śì-。pó-ba (shi 'pho ba), to exchange life, to die, (in the earlier literature the most common expression for it); the last of the above terms prob. may be explained by či-źiń 。pó-ba ('chi zhing 'pho ba); či-。pó-ba 。débs-pa ('chi 'pho ba 'debs pa), Thgr. frq. seems to mean: to help the soul to a happy departure. — 2. C. to fall out, to shed, of wheat and corn in general.

pf. and secondary form pog (phog), to hit, strike, touch, befal, meet, mńár-bai dris 。póg-pa tams-čád (mngar ba'i dris 'phog pa thams cad) all whom the sweet odour met, to whom it became perceptible Dzl.; gen. with la: 。od-zér ('od zer), gríb-ma mi-la 。póg (grib ma mi la 'phog), a ray of light, a shadow falls upon that man Glr. frq.; k̃ó-la nad (kho la nad), tsád-pa (tshad pa), čád-pa 。póg (chad pa 'phog), disease, heat, punishment etc. has befallen him; gza-。póg-mkan (gza' 'phog mkhan) an epileptic person W., C.; the signification: to hurt, seems to be less inherent to the word than dependent on contingent circumstances.
Cs. archery, 。póń - sa ('phong sa) archery ground, 。póń - mkan ('phong mkhan) archer, 。póń-skyén ('phong skyen) good, skilful archer Dzl.
= póń-tsos (phong tshos) Cs. buttocks; 。póńs ('phongs) sitting-part, posteriors Lt., Wdn.; 。póń-la skyón-pa ('phong la skyon pa) Sch. 'the riding of two persons on one horse'.

1. vb., pf. 。póńs ('phongs) or póńs (phongs), to be poor, indigent; 。póńs-par bźúg-pa ('phongs par bzhug pa) to let (another) pine in poverty Thgy.; with instrum. to be deprived of, to lose, rgyál-po srás-kyis 。póńs-nas (rgyal po sras kyis 'phongs nas) the king having lost his son Pth. — 2. also póńs-pa (phongs pa), sbst. poverty,
